rationale: Recent active-side CEX operational incident package for Bithumb. This is not a shutdown or hack record. Bithumb is modeled as an active centralized exchange with a February 2026 erroneous promotional credit / internal ledger incident, temporary affected-account restrictions, recovery of most erroneous balances, and regulatory inspection context.

entities.summary: South Korean centralized cryptocurrency exchange. In February 2026 it accidentally credited users with approximately 620,000 BTC in an internal promotional payout / ledger error, temporarily restricted affected accounts, recovered most erroneous balances, and came under regulatory inspection. The exchange remained active.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead or hacked. The February 2026 incident was reported as an internal ledger / promotional payout error rather than an external theft. Track it as a severe operational incident with affected-account restrictions and regulatory review while keeping the entity active.

events.title: Bithumb accidentally credited users with massive BTC balances
events.description: Bithumb mistakenly recorded approximately 620,000 BTC as promotional rewards in user accounts, a ledger/payout error widely reported as equivalent to tens of billions of U.S. dollars at the time.
events.notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no dedicated operational_error enum. This was not reported as a hack.

events.title: Bithumb restricted affected accounts after the crediting error
events.description: Bithumb restricted affected users' trading and withdrawals within about 35 minutes of the erroneous crediting incident and later recovered the vast majority of the mistaken balances.
events.notes: Modeled as withdrawal_suspended because the status-changing user impact involved account and withdrawal restrictions, not global exchange closure.

events.title: Bithumb incident prompted regulatory inspection
events.description: South Korean regulators and authorities were reported to be inspecting Bithumb after the erroneous BTC crediting incident exposed internal-control and operational-risk concerns.
events.notes: Regulatory inspection / scrutiny context; keep entity active.

evidence.title: Crypto firm accidentally sends $44 billion in bitcoins to users
evidence.notes: Primary source for the erroneous crediting incident, approximate BTC amount, USD equivalent, restrictions, and recovery percentage.

evidence.title: Bithumb mistakenly records 620,000 bitcoin token payout, exposes internal structural flaws
evidence.notes: Used for internal-control context, affected-account restrictions, recovery percentage, and regulatory scrutiny.

evidence.title: South Korean Cryptocurrency Exchange Bithumb Accidentally Sends $40 Billion In Bitcoin To Users
evidence.notes: Supporting source for scale of erroneous crediting and user-impact framing.

evidence.title: Bithumb mistakenly credited users with Bitcoin worth tens of billions of dollars
evidence.notes: Supporting Korean mainstream source for regulatory/inspection and operational-risk context.

manual_review_checklist: Confirm Bithumb is not already canonical before apply; if existing, update rather than duplicate.
Keep Bithumb active; this is an operational incident, not a shutdown or hack.
Use event ids hei_ev_000410 through hei_ev_000412 only if available.
Use evidence ids hei_src_000821 through hei_src_000824 only if available.
